summary: Hook Scoring Engine Testing Complete. Backend: 12/12 pytest tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI elements verified via Playwright. The Hybrid Hook Scoring Engine correctly implements Stage 1 (rule-based scoring) with detailed breakdown. Stage 2 (GPT scoring) is skipped due to LLM budget depletion.
critical_code_review_comments: Hook Scoring Engine correctly implements two-stage scoring: Stage 1 (rule-based, always works) and Stage 2 (GPT, skipped due to budget)
Rule-based scoring evaluates: length (0-15), first_line_hook (0-25), tension (0-20), cliffhanger (0-25), emotional_power (0-10), generic_penalty (-15), title_quality (0-5)
Auto-reject triggers: (hook_score < 5 AND cliff_score < 5) OR word_count < 15
Frontend correctly displays hook score badges with color coding: amber (HIGH), blue (MEDIUM), red (LOW)
Score All Hooks button triggers bulk scoring of unscored stories
VIDEO READY badge only appears for stories with hook_ready_for_video=true (score >= 70)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hook Scoring Engine testing complete. Key findings: (1) Backend APIs all working - score-hook returns detailed breakdown with rule_score, rule_tag, rule_breakdown, final_score, final_tag, ready_for_video, auto_reject, rejection_reasons, word_count, scoring_stages. (2) Strong hook story ('The Last Door') scored 70+ (HIGH), weak story ('A Nice Day') scored 0 (LOW) with generic penalty, medium story scored 40-69 (MEDIUM). (3) Rule engine correctly detects generic phrases ('once upon a time', 'happily ever after') and applies -15 penalty. (4) Rule engine rewards cliffhangers (stories ending with '...' or '—' get higher cliffhanger score). (5) Frontend shows hook score badges with color coding: amber=HIGH, blue=MEDIUM, red=LOW. (6) Score All Hooks button visible and functional. (7) VIDEO READY badge only appears for stories with score >= 70 (none currently). (8) GPT scoring (Stage 2) is skipped due to LLM budget depletion - use skip_gpt=true parameter.

Backend_Test_1_Score_Hook_Strong.details: POST /api/content-engine/score-hook with strong hook returns HIGH tag, score >= 70, ready_for_video=true

Backend_Test_2_Score_Hook_Weak.details: POST /api/content-engine/score-hook with weak story returns LOW tag, score < 40, generic_penalty applied

Backend_Test_3_Score_Hook_Medium.details: POST /api/content-engine/score-hook with decent story returns MEDIUM tag, score 40-69

Backend_Test_4_Score_All.details: POST /api/content-engine/score-all scores all unscored stories, returns breakdown (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW counts)

Backend_Test_5_Score_Existing.details: POST /api/content-engine/score-existing/{story_id} scores and updates story record

Backend_Test_6_Generic_Phrases.details: Rule engine detects 'once upon a time', 'happily ever after' and applies negative generic_penalty

Backend_Test_7_Cliffhanger_Reward.details: Stories ending with '...' or '—' get higher cliffhanger score (>= 10)

Frontend_Test_1_Hook_Scores.details: Story cards show HOOK {score} badge with color coding: amber (HIGH), blue (MEDIUM), red (LOW)

Frontend_Test_2_Score_All_Button.details: Score All Hooks button visible in header, clickable, shows toast notification on completion

Frontend_Test_3_VIDEO_READY_Badge.details: VIDEO READY badge logic verified - only appears for stories with hook_ready_for_video=true (score >= 70). Currently 0 badges shown because all stories score < 70

mocked_apis.note: All APIs are real. GPT scoring (Stage 2) is skipped due to LLM budget depletion - use skip_gpt=true parameter. Rule-based scoring (Stage 1) works without any API calls.

hook_scoring_breakdown.generic_penalty: -15 points max (generic phrases like 'once upon a time')
